data.title: Korn Ferry Likely To Report Higher Q3 Earnings; These Most Accurate Analysts Revise Forecasts Ahead Of Earnings Call
data.description: Before the market opens on Monday, March 9, Korn Ferry (NYSE: KFY) is set to report its third-quarter earnings. Analysts predict earnings per share of $1.24, a rise from $1.19 in the same period last year. Revenue for the quarter is anticipated to be around $695.12 million, compared to $668.73 million in the previous year. The company recently increased its quarterly dividend from 48 cents to 55 cents per share on March 5. Korn Ferry's stock price climbed 3% to $65.08 at the close of trading on Thursday. For more detailed information and latest analyst ratings, readers can refer to the Analyst Stock Ratings page.

data.title: Quality Used Machinery Inventory Shrinks as Equipment Shifts Into Rental Fleets
data.description: In Lincoln, Nebraska on March 6, 2026, recent reports from Sandhills Global indicate a decrease in inventory levels of used construction equipment on various platforms including MachineryTrader.com and LiftsToday.com. However, forklifts have seen an increase in availability. This trend is reflected across all markets, with a surge in demand for construction equipment. The numbers show a clear decline in inventory for all categories except for forklifts, signifying a noteworthy shift in the market dynamics.

data.title: DraftKings and ESPN Detail Plans to Provide Account Linking for Customers
data.description: In Boston, DraftKings Inc. (NASDAQ: DKNG) and ESPN revealed plans at the MIT Sloan Sports Analytics Conference for account linking between DraftKings' leading Sportsbook and ESPN. This collaboration allows customers to enhance their experience by connecting their accounts, granting them access to unique features like the upcoming "Bet Your Bracket" service. The account linking feature is set to debut prior to the March Madness tournaments for both men and women, bringing users a more tailored and efficient interaction.

data.title: Knights of Columbus Distributes Record-Breaking 300,000th Coat to Children in Need on Historic, 900 km-Journey to Cree First Nations Community in Northern Manitoba
data.description: In Pukatawagan, Manitoba, the Knights of Columbus recently provided a significant contribution to the Cree First Nations community. A group of Knights traveled through difficult winter conditions to deliver aid to the village, home to around 2,000 people. The donation included 500 new winter coats for children, 22 high-quality beds, and 10 wheelchairs for individuals in need of healthcare assistance. This generous gesture showcases the Knights of Columbus' commitment to supporting communities in need.

data.title: OpenData.org Launches Comprehensive U.S. Entity Dataset with Senzing AI
data.description: In Las Vegas, OpenData.org, the leading global entity graph, has unveiled a new U.S. dataset containing a vast amount of information. The dataset comprises 86 million organizations, 101 million contacts (representing relationships between individuals and companies), and 142 million locations. Through a collaboration with Senzing.ai, a renowned provider of entity resolution solutions, the dataset will be offered in Senzing-ready JSON format. This release is a significant milestone as it offers a comprehensive perspective on the U.S. business landscape.

data.title: Entera Bio Submits Streamlined Phase 3 Protocol to Initiate Registrational Program for EB613 in Postmenopausal Women with Osteoporosis
data.description: A Phase 3 clinical trial has been structured to evaluate the primary endpoint of total hip bone mineral density (BMD) at 12 months instead of the originally planned 24 months. This study aims to assess the impact of the treatment on bone health, with a focus on the hip area. The shortened timeline for evaluating the primary endpoint may expedite the overall study process and provide earlier insights into the efficacy of the treatment.

data.title: KBR Wins 10-Year Contract To Power Indorama's Global Ammonia Operations
data.description: KBR Inc. (NYSE: KBR) recently secured a 10-year catalyst supply agreement from Indorama Eleme Fertilizer & Chemicals FZE, enhancing operational efficiency across its ammonia plant portfolio. This marks KBR's first long-term catalyst deal in the ammonia sector. The contract involves providing catalyst solutions for Indorama's six ammonia plants in Nigeria, Georgia, Uzbekistan, and India. Leveraging KBR's expertise in ammonia design and operations, the deal aims to optimize efficiency and reliability for Indorama. Presently, KBR is trading 8.5% lower than its 20-day simple moving average.

data.title: Trump brings Warren and Scott together — against Wall Street
data.description: Lawmakers in Congress are making progress on a housing affordability bill, marking the first of its kind in 10 years. The development demonstrates how bipartisan cooperation is functioning in the current political climate under the Trump administration.

data.title: Circadence Closes $16.4 Million Investment Round Amid Strong Revenue Growth and Launch of RangeGPT and Project Ares GEN3
data.description: Circadence, based in Boulder, Colorado, recently completed a $16.4 million funding round with Seneca Partners as the lead investor. This investment emphasizes Circadence's role as a top player in AI-based cybersecurity readiness and resilience. The company has seen substantial revenue growth and increased uptake in defense and enterprise sectors. The funding coincides with the introduction of RangeGPT and Project Ares GEN3, a consolidated AI-driven platform revolutionizing organizations' approach to modern digital challenges.

data.title: VEON’s Beeline Uzbekistan and Rakuten Symphony  Partner for Open RAN, AI Collaboration
data.description: On March 4, 2026, VEON Ltd. (Nasdaq: VEON) and Rakuten Group, Inc. revealed a partnership between Beeline Uzbekistan, VEON’s operator in Uzbekistan, and Rakuten Symphony, a Rakuten Group subsidiary. This collaboration aims to foster Uzbekistan’s rapidly expanding digital economy. The Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) signed by the two entities outlines plans for joint efforts on Open RAN advancement, utilization of AI for network management, and enhancing next-gen digital systems. The deal signifies a strategic move to capitalize on the digital growth wave.

data.title: Dow Jones Futures Waver On Trump Tariff Hike, Iran Outreach Report
data.description: Following an initial decline, futures saw a rise following reports of Iran's communication with the U.S. However, there is anticipation of a tariff increase by President Trump this week as stated by Treasury Secretary Bessent. The Dow Jones Futures are showing uncertainty in response to these developments.

data.title: Job report shows weak numbers, Fed dilemma as war fuels inflation risk. Rates likely to remain stable at March 17-18 meeting.
data.description: There is a prevailing expectation that the Federal Reserve will maintain current interest rates during their upcoming meeting on March 17-18.

data.title: Stock Market Today: Dow Loses 400 Points Amid U.S.-Iran Battle; Palantir Climbs As Nvidia Slips (Live Coverage)
data.description: Today, the Dow Jones index experienced a significant decline of 400 points amidst escalating tensions between the U.S. and Iran. Meanwhile, Palantir saw an increase in its stock value, while Nvidia faced a decline. The market fluctuations were driven by the geopolitical uncertainties surrounding the U.S.-Iran conflict.

data.title: Siren Fusion 2026: The Convergence of People, Technology and Ideas
data.description: In Galway, Ireland, Siren has unveiled the Siren Fusion 2026, a global summit gathering senior investigators, national security leaders, and AI innovators for discussions on investigative intelligence. This summit aims to bring together individuals from various backgrounds to discuss the future of investigative intelligence. Fusion 2026, designed as an educational and strategic forum rather than a standard conference, emphasizes the synergy of people, technology, and ideas within the realm of national security. This event, first of its kind, will be held in Galway, Ireland, offering a high-trust environment for thought-provoking discussions.
